title: anthyllis montana var. genuina
intro: Anthyllis montana var. genuina, also known as Alpine Kidney Vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, characterized by its pink or white flowers and kidney-shaped pods. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing pain and inflammation. The health benefits of Anthyllis montana var. genuina include its ability to support the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
